root_text.sn35.62:4.1: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vā taṁ sukhaṁ vā”ti?
root_text.sn35.62:6.1: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vipariṇāmadhammaṁ, kallaṁ nu taṁ samanupassituṁ:
root_text.sn35.62:12.1: “Yampidaṁ cakkhusamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi niccaṁ vā aniccaṁ vā”ti?
root_text.sn35.62:14.9: yampidaṁ manosamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā, tampi niccaṁ vā aniccaṁ vā”ti?
root_text.sn35.62:16.1: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vā taṁ sukhaṁ vā”ti?
root_text.sn35.62:18.1: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vipariṇāmadhammaṁ, kallaṁ nu taṁ samanupassituṁ:
root_text.sn35.62:20.1: “Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako cakkhusmimpi nibbindati, rūpesupi nibbindati, cakkhuviññāṇepi nibbindati, cakkhusamphassepi nibbindati.
root_text.sn35.62:20.2: Yampidaṁ cakkhusamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tasmimpi nibbindati …pe…
root_text.sn35.62:20.3: jivhāyapi nibbindati, rasesupi nibbindati, jivhāviññāṇepi nibbindati, jivhāsamphassepi nibbindati, yampidaṁ jivhāsamphassapaccayā uppajjati …pe…
root_text.sn35.62:20.4: manasmimpi nibbindati, dhammesupi nibbindati, manoviññāṇepi nibbindati, manosamphassepi nibbindati.
root_text.sn35.62:20.5: Yampidaṁ manosamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tasmimpi nibbindati.
root_text.sn35.62:20.6: Nibbindaṁ virajjati; virāgā vimuccati; vimuttasmiṁ vimuttamiti ñāṇaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn35.62:20.7: ‘Khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānāti.

translation_text.sn35.62:0.3: The Depletion of All Fuel for Grasping (2nd)
translation_text.sn35.62:1.1: “Mendicants, I will teach you the principle for depleting all fuel for grasping.
translation_text.sn35.62:1.3: And what is the principle for depleting all fuel for grasping?
translation_text.sn35.62:2.2: Is the eye permanent or impermanent?”
translation_text.sn35.62:4.1: “But if it’s impermanent, is it suffering or happiness?”
translation_text.sn35.62:6.1: “But if it’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable, is it fit to be regarded thus:
translation_text.sn35.62:6.2: ‘This is mine, I am this, this is my self’?”
translation_text.sn35.62:12.1: The pleasant, painful, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on eye contact: is that permanent or impermanent?”
translation_text.sn35.62:14.9: The pleasant, painful, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on mind contact: is that permanent or impermanent?”
translation_text.sn35.62:16.1: “But if it’s impermanent, is it suffering or happiness?”
translation_text.sn35.62:18.1: “But if it’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable, is it fit to be regarded thus:
translation_text.sn35.62:18.2: ‘This is mine, I am this, this is my self’?”
translation_text.sn35.62:20.1: “Seeing this, a learned noble disciple grows disillusioned with the eye, sights, eye consciousness, and eye contact.
translation_text.sn35.62:20.2: And they grow disillusioned with the painful, pleasant, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on eye contact.
translation_text.sn35.62:20.3: They grow disillusioned with the ear … nose … tongue … body …
translation_text.sn35.62:20.4: They grow disillusioned with the mind, ideas, mind consciousness, and mind contact.
translation_text.sn35.62:20.5: And they grow disillusioned with the painful, pleasant, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on mind contact.
translation_text.sn35.62:20.6: Being disillusioned, desire fades away. When desire fades away they’re freed. When they’re freed, they know they’re freed.
translation_text.sn35.62:20.7: They understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’
translation_text.sn35.62:20.8: This is the principle for depleting all fuel for grasping.”
